27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	# Helper: serialize a Role into a JSON-safe dict
29	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	
31	def _serialize_role(role):
32	    """Convert a Role ORM object into a plain dict for JSON responses.
33	
34	    Uses list comprehension for permission extraction (Sample 2 style).
35	    """
36	    return {
37	        'id': role.id,
38	        'name': role.name,
39	        'tenant_id': role.tenant_id,
40	        'parent_role_id': role.parent_role_id,
41	        'permissions': [p.code for p in role.permissions],
42	    }
43	
44	
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	# Helper: iterative BFS cycle detection
47	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
48	
49	def _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
50	    """Check whether setting role_id's parent to new_parent_id creates a cycle.
51	
52	    Uses an iterative BFS traversal starting from new_parent_id, walking up
53	    the parent chain. If we ever reach role_id, a cycle would form.
54	
55	    Returns True if a cycle would be created, False otherwise.
56	    """
57	    if new_parent_id is None:
58	        return False
59	
60	    visited = set()
61	    queue = deque([new_parent_id])
62	
63	    while queue:
64	        current_id = queue.popleft()
65	        if current_id == role_id:
66	            return True
67	        if current_id in visited:
68	            continue
69	        visited.add(current_id)
70	
71	        current_role = Role.query.get(current_id)
72	        if current_role and current_role.parent_role_id is not None:
73	            queue.append(current_role.parent_role_id)
74	
75	    return False
76	
77	
78	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
79	# POST /roles -- Create Role
80	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
81	
82	@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['POST'])
83	@require_auth
84	def create_role():
85	    """Create a new role within the authenticated user's tenant.
86	
87	    Request body:
88	        {"name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}
89	
90	    Requires role.manage permission.
91	    """
92	    # Inline permission check (Sample 2: manual, not decorator)
93	    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
94	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403
95	
96	    body = request.json
97	
98	    # Validate: name is required
99	    if not body or not isinstance(body.get('name'), str) or not body['name'].strip():
100	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing or invalid name'}), 400
101	
102	    current_user = g.current_user
103	    tenant_id = current_user.tenant_id
104	    name = body['name'].strip()
105	
106	    # Duplicate name check within same tenant
107	    existing = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name).first()
108	    if existing:
109	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role name already exists in this tenant'}), 409
110	
111	    # Validate parent_role_id if provided
112	    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
113	    if parent_role_id is not None:
114	        parent = Role.query.get(parent_role_id)
115	        if parent is None or parent.tenant_id != tenant_id:
116	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Invalid parent role'}), 400
117	
118	    # Create role
119	    new_role = Role(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name, parent_role_id=parent_role_id)
120	    db.session.add(new_role)
121	    db.session.flush()  # flush to get the id before adding permissions
122	
123	    # Add permissions one-by-one (Sample 2 style, not bulk insert)
124	    perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
125	    for code in perm_codes:
126	        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
127	        if perm:
128	            new_role.permissions.append(perm)
129	
130	    db.session.commit()
131	
132	    return jsonify({
133	        'status': 'ok',
134	        'data': _serialize_role(new_role),
135	    }), 201
136	
137

139	# GET /roles -- List Roles
140	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

143	@require_auth
144	def list_roles():
145	    """List all roles belonging to the authenticated user's tenant.
146	
147	    Requires role.manage permission.
148	    """
149	    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
150	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403
151	
152	    tenant_id = g.current_user.tenant_id
153	    roles = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id).all()
154	
155	    # Serialize using list comprehension (Sample 2 style)
156	    data = [_serialize_role(r) for r in roles]
157	
158	    return jsonify({
159	        'status': 'ok',
160	        'data': data,
161	    }), 200
162	
163	
164	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
165	# PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Update Permissions & Parent
166	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
167	
168	@role_bp.route('/roles/<int:role_id>/permissions', methods=['PUT'])
169	@require_auth
170	def update_role_permissions(role_id):
171	    """Replace a role's permission set and optionally update its parent.
172	
173	    Request body:
174	        {"permissions": [str, ...], "parent_role_id": int|null}
175	
176	    Detects inheritance cycles when parent_role_id is changed.
177	    Requires role.manage permission.
178	    """
179	    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
180	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403
181	
182	    role = Role.query.get(role_id)
183	    if role is None:
184	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404
185	
186	    # Tenant isolation: only manage roles in own tenant
187	    if role.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
188	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404
189	
190	    body = request.json
191	    if not body:
192	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be JSON'}), 400
193	
194	    # Handle parent_role_id update with cycle detection
195	    new_parent_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
196	    if new_parent_id is not None:
197	        parent = Role.query.get(new_parent_id)
198	        if parent is None or parent.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
199	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Invalid parent role'}), 400
200	
201	        if _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
202	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Cycle detected in role inheritance'}), 400
203	
204	    # Update parent
205	    role.parent_role_id = new_parent_id
206	
207	    # Replace permissions: clear existing, add new ones one-by-one
208	    role.permissions.clear()
209	
210	    new_perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
211	    for code in new_perm_codes:
212	        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
213	        if perm:
214	            role.permissions.append(perm)
215	
216	    db.session.commit()
217	
218	    return jsonify({
219	        'status': 'ok',

221	    }), 200

253	
254	    target_role = Role.query.get(body['role_id'])

261

"""
T2 RBAC System -- Role Management Routes (Sample 2 Variant)

Evolutionary mutation from Sample 1 with deliberate implementation differences:
- Iterative BFS for cycle detection (not recursive DFS)
- Inline permission checks using require_auth + manual g.current_permissions lookup
- Direct request.json access (not get_json(silent=True))
- One-by-one permission association insert (not bulk SQL)
- List comprehension serialization for role data
- Inline input parsing within route handlers (no extracted helpers)

Register as a Flask Blueprint named 'role_bp'.
All operations require role.manage permission.
"""

from collections import deque

from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g

from models import User, Role, Permission, user_roles, role_permissions
from middleware import require_auth
from app import db

role_bp = Blueprint('role_bp', __name__)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: serialize a Role into a JSON-safe dict
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _serialize_role(role):
    """Convert a Role ORM object into a plain dict for JSON responses.

    Uses list comprehension for permission extraction (Sample 2 style).
    """
    return {
        'id': role.id,
        'name': role.name,
        'tenant_id': role.tenant_id,
        'parent_role_id': role.parent_role_id,
        'permissions': [p.code for p in role.permissions],
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: iterative BFS cycle detection
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
    """Check whether setting role_id's parent to new_parent_id creates a cycle.

    Uses an iterative BFS traversal starting from new_parent_id, walking up
    the parent chain. If we ever reach role_id, a cycle would form.

    Returns True if a cycle would be created, False otherwise.
    """
    if new_parent_id is None:
        return False

    visited = set()
    queue = deque([new_parent_id])

    while queue:
        current_id = queue.popleft()
        if current_id == role_id:
            return True
        if current_id in visited:
            continue
        visited.add(current_id)

        current_role = Role.query.get(current_id)
        if current_role and current_role.parent_role_id is not None:
            queue.append(current_role.parent_role_id)

    return False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POST /roles -- Create Role
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['POST'])
@require_auth
def create_role():
    """Create a new role within the authenticated user's tenant.

    Request body:
        {"name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}

    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    # Inline permission check (Sample 2: manual, not decorator)
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    body = request.json

    # Validate: name is required
    if not body or not isinstance(body.get('name'), str) or not body['name'].strip():
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing or invalid name'}), 400

    current_user = g.current_user
    tenant_id = current_user.tenant_id
    name = body['name'].strip()

    # Duplicate name check within same tenant
    existing = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name).first()
    if existing:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role name already exists in this tenant'}), 409

    # Validate parent_role_id if provided
    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
    if parent_role_id is not None:
        parent = Role.query.get(parent_role_id)
        if parent is None or parent.tenant_id != tenant_id:
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Invalid parent role'}), 400

    # Create role
    new_role = Role(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name, parent_role_id=parent_role_id)
    db.session.add(new_role)
    db.session.flush()  # flush to get the id before adding permissions

    # Add permissions one-by-one (Sample 2 style, not bulk insert)
    perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
    for code in perm_codes:
        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
        if perm:
            new_role.permissions.append(perm)

    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': _serialize_role(new_role),
    }), 201


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GET /roles -- List Roles
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['GET'])
@require_auth
def list_roles():
    """List all roles belonging to the authenticated user's tenant.

    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    tenant_id = g.current_user.tenant_id
    roles = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id).all()

    # Serialize using list comprehension (Sample 2 style)
    data = [_serialize_role(r) for r in roles]

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': data,
    }), 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Update Permissions & Parent
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/roles/<int:role_id>/permissions', methods=['PUT'])
@require_auth
def update_role_permissions(role_id):
    """Replace a role's permission set and optionally update its parent.

    Request body:
        {"permissions": [str, ...], "parent_role_id": int|null}

    Detects inheritance cycles when parent_role_id is changed.
    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    role = Role.query.get(role_id)
    if role is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    # Tenant isolation: only manage roles in own tenant
    if role.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    body = request.json
    if not body:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be JSON'}), 400

    # Handle parent_role_id update with cycle detection
    new_parent_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
    if new_parent_id is not None:
        parent = Role.query.get(new_parent_id)
        if parent is None or parent.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Invalid parent role'}), 400

        if _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Cycle detected in role inheritance'}), 400

    # Update parent
    role.parent_role_id = new_parent_id

    # Replace permissions: clear existing, add new ones one-by-one
    role.permissions.clear()

    new_perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
    for code in new_perm_codes:
        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
        if perm:
            role.permissions.append(perm)

    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': _serialize_role(role),
    }), 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POST /users/<id>/roles -- Assign Role to User
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles', methods=['POST'])
@require_auth
def assign_role(user_id):
    """Assign a role to a user.

    Request body:
        {"role_id": int}

    Both user and role must belong to the same tenant as the authenticated user.
    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    body = request.json
    if not body or not isinstance(body.get('role_id'), int):
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing or invalid role_id'}), 400

    target_user = User.query.get(user_id)
    if target_user is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    # Cross-tenant isolation
    if target_user.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    target_role = Role.query.get(body['role_id'])
    if target_role is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    # Cross-tenant isolation for role
    if target_role.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    # Assign via association table insert (ignore if already assigned)
    existing = db.session.execute(
        user_roles.select().where(
            (user_roles.c.user_id == user_id) & (user_roles.c.role_id == target_role.id)
        )
    ).fetchone()

    if not existing:
        db.session.execute(
            user_roles.insert().values(user_id=user_id, role_id=target_role.id)
        )

    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': {'user_id': user_id, 'role_id': target_role.id},
    }), 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DELETE /users/<id>/roles/<role_id> -- Remove Role from User
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles/<int:role_id>', methods=['DELETE'])
@require_auth
def remove_role(user_id, role_id):
    """Remove a role assignment from a user.

    Idempotent: returns 200 even if the role was not assigned to the user.
    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    target_user = User.query.get(user_id)
    if target_user is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    # Cross-tenant isolation
    if target_user.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    # Delete the association if it exists (idempotent)
    db.session.execute(
        user_roles.delete().where(
            (user_roles.c.user_id == user_id) & (user_roles.c.role_id == role_id)
        )
    )

    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': {'user_id': user_id, 'role_id': role_id},
    }), 200

"""
T2 RBAC System -- Role Management Routes (Sample 2 Variant)

Evolutionary mutation from Sample 1 with deliberate implementation differences:
- Iterative BFS for cycle detection (not recursive DFS)
- Inline permission checks using require_auth + manual g.current_permissions lookup
- Direct request.json access (not get_json(silent=True))
- One-by-one permission association insert (not bulk SQL)
- List comprehension serialization for role data
- Inline input parsing within route handlers (no extracted helpers)

Register as a Flask Blueprint named 'role_bp'.
All operations require role.manage permission.
"""

from collections import deque

from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g

from models import User, Role, Permission, user_roles, role_permissions
from middleware import require_auth
from app import db

role_bp = Blueprint('role_bp', __name__)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: serialize a Role into a JSON-safe dict
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _serialize_role(role):
    """Convert a Role ORM object into a plain dict for JSON responses.

    Uses list comprehension for permission extraction (Sample 2 style).
    """
    return {
        'id': role.id,
        'name': role.name,
        'tenant_id': role.tenant_id,
        'parent_role_id': role.parent_role_id,
        'permissions': [p.code for p in role.permissions],
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper: iterative BFS cycle detection
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
    """Check whether setting role_id's parent to new_parent_id creates a cycle.

    Uses an iterative BFS traversal starting from new_parent_id, walking up
    the parent chain. If we ever reach role_id, a cycle would form.

    Returns True if a cycle would be created, False otherwise.
    """
    if new_parent_id is None:
        return False

    visited = set()
    queue = deque([new_parent_id])

    while queue:
        current_id = queue.popleft()
        if current_id == role_id:
            return True
        if current_id in visited:
            continue
        visited.add(current_id)

        current_role = Role.query.get(current_id)
        if current_role and current_role.parent_role_id is not None:
            queue.append(current_role.parent_role_id)

    return False


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POST /roles -- Create Role
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['POST'])
@require_auth
def create_role():
    """Create a new role within the authenticated user's tenant.

    Request body:
        {"name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}

    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    # Inline permission check (Sample 2: manual, not decorator)
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    body = request.json

    # Validate: name is required
    if not body or not isinstance(body.get('name'), str) or not body['name'].strip():
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing or invalid name'}), 400

    current_user = g.current_user
    tenant_id = current_user.tenant_id
    name = body['name'].strip()

    # Duplicate name check within same tenant
    existing = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name).first()
    if existing:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role name already exists in this tenant'}), 409

    # Validate parent_role_id if provided
    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
    if parent_role_id is not None:
        parent = Role.query.get(parent_role_id)
        if parent is None or parent.tenant_id != tenant_id:
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Invalid parent role'}), 400

    # Create role
    new_role = Role(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name, parent_role_id=parent_role_id)
    db.session.add(new_role)
    db.session.flush()  # flush to get the id before adding permissions

    # Add permissions one-by-one (Sample 2 style, not bulk insert)
    perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
    for code in perm_codes:
        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
        if perm:
            new_role.permissions.append(perm)

    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': _serialize_role(new_role),
    }), 201


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# GET /roles -- List Roles
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['GET'])
@require_auth
def list_roles():
    """List all roles belonging to the authenticated user's tenant.

    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    tenant_id = g.current_user.tenant_id
    roles = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id).all()

    # Serialize using list comprehension (Sample 2 style)
    data = [_serialize_role(r) for r in roles]

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': data,
    }), 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Update Permissions & Parent
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/roles/<int:role_id>/permissions', methods=['PUT'])
@require_auth
def update_role_permissions(role_id):
    """Replace a role's permission set and optionally update its parent.

    Request body:
        {"permissions": [str, ...], "parent_role_id": int|null}

    Detects inheritance cycles when parent_role_id is changed.
    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    role = Role.query.get(role_id)
    if role is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    # Tenant isolation: only manage roles in own tenant
    if role.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    body = request.json
    if not body:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be JSON'}), 400

    # Handle parent_role_id update with cycle detection
    new_parent_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
    if new_parent_id is not None:
        parent = Role.query.get(new_parent_id)
        if parent is None or parent.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Invalid parent role'}), 400

        if _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Cycle detected in role inheritance'}), 400

    # Update parent
    role.parent_role_id = new_parent_id

    # Replace permissions: clear existing, add new ones one-by-one
    role.permissions.clear()

    new_perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
    for code in new_perm_codes:
        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
        if perm:
            role.permissions.append(perm)

    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': _serialize_role(role),
    }), 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# POST /users/<id>/roles -- Assign Role to User
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles', methods=['POST'])
@require_auth
def assign_role(user_id):
    """Assign a role to a user.

    Request body:
        {"role_id": int}

    Both user and role must belong to the same tenant as the authenticated user.
    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    body = request.json
    if not body or not isinstance(body.get('role_id'), int):
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing or invalid role_id'}), 400

    target_user = User.query.get(user_id)
    if target_user is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    # Cross-tenant isolation
    if target_user.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    target_role = Role.query.get(body['role_id'])
    if target_role is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    # Cross-tenant isolation for role
    if target_role.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    # Assign via association table insert (ignore if already assigned)
    existing = db.session.execute(
        user_roles.select().where(
            (user_roles.c.user_id == user_id) & (user_roles.c.role_id == target_role.id)
        )
    ).fetchone()

    if not existing:
        db.session.execute(
            user_roles.insert().values(user_id=user_id, role_id=target_role.id)
        )

    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': {'user_id': user_id, 'role_id': target_role.id},
    }), 200


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# DELETE /users/<id>/roles/<role_id> -- Remove Role from User
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles/<int:role_id>', methods=['DELETE'])
@require_auth
def remove_role(user_id, role_id):
    """Remove a role assignment from a user.

    Idempotent: returns 200 even if the role was not assigned to the user.
    Requires role.manage permission.
    """
    if 'role.manage' not in g.current_permissions:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Permission denied'}), 403

    target_user = User.query.get(user_id)
    if target_user is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    # Cross-tenant isolation
    if target_user.tenant_id != g.current_user.tenant_id:

    # Delete the association if it exists (idempotent)

1	"""
2	T2 RBAC System -- Role Management Routes (Gen 1 Sample 1 Variant)
3	
4	Evolutionary approach: recursive cycle detection, independent permission
5	resolution helper, batch permission insertion via association table inserts.
6	
7	Register as a Flask Blueprint named 'role_bp'.
8	All operations require role.manage permission.
9	
10	Key design decisions (Sample 1 signature):
11	- Cycle detection uses a recursive _would_create_cycle() function that
12	  walks the parent chain from new_parent_id looking for role_id
13	- Permission resolution is extracted into _resolve_permissions() which
14	  maps permission codes to Permission IDs with validation
15	- Batch inserts via db.session.execute(role_permissions.insert())
16	- Helper functions return (data, data, error_response) tuples for clean
17	  separation of validation from business logic
18	"""
19	
20	from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g
21	from sqlalchemy import select, and_
22	
23	from models import Role, Permission, User, role_permissions, user_roles
24	from middleware import check_permission
25	from app import db
26	
27	role_bp = Blueprint('role_bp', __name__)
28	
29	
30	# ============================================================
31	# Helper Functions
32	# ============================================================
33	
34	
35	def _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
36	    """Check if setting new_parent_id as parent of role_id would create a cycle.
37	
38	    Walks up the parent chain from new_parent_id. If we encounter role_id,
39	    then making new_parent_id the parent of role_id would form a cycle.
40	
41	    Args:
42	        role_id: The role being updated (the potential child).
43	        new_parent_id: The proposed new parent role.
44	
45	    Returns:
46	        True if a cycle would be created, False otherwise.
47	    """
48	    if new_parent_id is None:
49	        return False
50	
51	    visited = set()
52	    current_id = new_parent_id
53	
54	    while current_id is not None:
55	        if current_id == role_id:
56	            return True
57	        if current_id in visited:
58	            # Already visited -- should not happen in valid data, but safety guard
59	            return True
60	        visited.add(current_id)
61	
62	        role = db.session.get(Role, current_id)
63	        if role is None:
64	            break
65	        current_id = role.parent_role_id
66	
67	    return False
68	
69	
70	def _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id=None):
71	    """Map permission code strings to Permission model instances.
72	
73	    Looks up each permission code in the database. Validates that all
74	    codes correspond to existing permissions.
75	
76	    Args:
77	        perm_codes: List of permission code strings (e.g. ['doc.read', 'doc.write']).
78	        tenant_id: Unused parameter kept for API consistency with potential
79	                   future tenant-scoped permissions.
80	
81	    Returns:
82	        (permissions, error_response) tuple.
83	        On success: (list of Permission objects, None)
84	        On failure: (None, (jsonify response, status_code))
85	    """
86	    if not isinstance(perm_codes, list):
87	        return None, (jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'permissions must be a list'}), 400)
88	
89	    permissions = []
90	    for code in perm_codes:
91	        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
92	        if perm is None:
93	            return None, (
94	                jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': f'Unknown permission: {code}'}),
95	                400,
96	            )
97	        permissions.append(perm)
98	
99	    return permissions, None
100	
101	
102	def _serialize_role(role):
103	    """Serialize a Role model instance into a JSON-safe dict.
104	
105	    Includes id, name, tenant_id, parent_role_id, and permission codes.
106	    """
107	    return {
108	        'id': role.id,
109	        'name': role.name,
110	        'tenant_id': role.tenant_id,
111	        'parent_role_id': role.parent_role_id,
112	        'permissions': [p.code for p in role.permissions],
113	    }
114	
115	
116	def _get_current_tenant_id():
117	    """Extract tenant_id from the authenticated user stored in g."""
118	    return g.current_user.tenant_id
119	
120	
121	# ============================================================
122	# POST /roles -- Create Role
123	# ============================================================
124	
125	
126	@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['POST'])
127	@check_permission('role.manage')
128	def create_role():
129	    """POST /roles -- Create a new role within the current tenant.
130	
131	    Request body (JSON):
132	        {"name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}
133	
134	    Success (201):
135	        {"status": "ok", "data": {"id": int, "name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null,
136	                  "permissions": [str, ...]}}
137	
138	    Errors:
139	        400 -- Missing name, invalid parent, or unknown permissions
140	        403 -- Insufficient permissions
141	        409 -- Duplicate role name within the same tenant
142	    """
143	    body = request.get_json(silent=True)
144	
145	    # Step 1: Validate request body structure
146	    if not isinstance(body, dict):
147	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be a JSON object'}), 400
148	
149	    name = body.get('name')
150	    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
151	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing or invalid name'}), 400
152	
153	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
154	
155	    # Step 2: Check for duplicate name within the same tenant
156	    existing = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name).first()
157	    if existing is not None:
158	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role name already exists in this tenant'}), 409
159	
160	    # Step 3: Validate parent_role_id if provided
161	    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
162	    if parent_role_id is not None:
163	        parent_role = db.session.get(Role, parent_role_id)
164	        if parent_role is None:
165	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role not found'}), 400
166	        if parent_role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
167	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role must belong to the same tenant'}), 400
168	
169	    # Step 4: Resolve permissions
170	    perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
171	    permissions, perm_error = _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id)
172	    if perm_error:
173	        return perm_error
174	
175	    # Step 5: Create the role
176	    role = Role(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name, parent_role_id=parent_role_id)
177	    db.session.add(role)
178	    db.session.flush()  # Flush to get role.id for permission insertion
179	
180	    # Step 6: Batch insert permissions via association table
181	    if permissions:
182	        db.session.execute(
183	            role_permissions.insert().values(
184	                [(role.id, p.id) for p in permissions]
185	            )
186	        )
187	
188	    db.session.commit()
189	
190	    return jsonify({
191	        'status': 'ok',
192	        'data': _serialize_role(role),
193	    }), 201
194	
195	
196	# ============================================================
197	# GET /roles -- List Roles
198	# ============================================================
199	
200	
201	@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['GET'])
202	@check_permission('role.manage')
203	def list_roles():
204	    """GET /roles -- List all roles for the current tenant.
205	
206	    Success (200):
207	        {"status": "ok", "data": [{"id": int, "name": str, "tenant_id": int,
208	                  "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}, ...]}
209	
210	    Errors:
211	        403 -- Insufficient permissions
212	    """
213	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
214	    roles = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id).all()
215	
216	    return jsonify({
217	        'status': 'ok',
218	        'data': [_serialize_role(role) for role in roles],
219	    }), 200
220	
221	
222	# ============================================================
223	# PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Update Permissions & Parent
224	# ============================================================
225	
226	
227	@role_bp.route('/roles/<int:role_id>/permissions', methods=['PUT'])
228	@check_permission('role.manage')
229	def update_role_permissions(role_id):
230	    """PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Replace permission set and update parent.
231	
232	    Request body (JSON):
233	        {"permissions": [str, ...], "parent_role_id": int|null}
234	
235	    Success (200):
236	        {"status": "ok", "data": {"id": int, "name": str, "tenant_id": int,
237	                  "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}}
238	
239	    Errors:
240	        400 -- Cycle detected, invalid parent, or unknown permissions
241	        403 -- Insufficient permissions
242	        404 -- Role not found
243	    """
244	    role = db.session.get(Role, role_id)
245	    if role is None:
246	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404
247	
248	    body = request.get_json(silent=True)
249	
250	    # Step 1: Validate request body structure
251	    if not isinstance(body, dict):
252	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be a JSON object'}), 400
253	
254	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
255	
256	    # Step 2: Handle parent_role_id update if provided
257	    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
258	    if parent_role_id is not None:
259	        # Validate parent exists and belongs to same tenant
260	        parent_role = db.session.get(Role, parent_role_id)
261	        if parent_role is None:
262	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role not found'}), 400
263	        if parent_role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
264	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role must belong to the same tenant'}), 400
265	
266	        # Check for inheritance cycle
267	        if _would_create_cycle(role_id, parent_role_id):
268	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Setting this parent would create an inheritance cycle'}), 400
269	
270	        role.parent_role_id = parent_role_id
271	    elif 'parent_role_id' in body:
272	        # Explicitly set to null if key is present but value is None
273	        role.parent_role_id = None
274	
275	    # Step 3: Resolve new permissions if provided
276	    perm_codes = body.get('permissions')
277	    if perm_codes is not None:
278	        permissions, perm_error = _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id)
279	        if perm_error:
280	            return perm_error
281	
282	        # Delete existing permissions and insert new ones
283	        db.session.execute(
284	            role_permissions.delete().where(role_permissions.c.role_id == role_id)
285	        )
286	        if permissions:
287	            db.session.execute(
288	                role_permissions.insert().values(
289	                    [(role_id, p.id) for p in permissions]
290	                )
291	            )
292	
293	    db.session.commit()
294	
295	    # Refresh role to pick up updated permissions
296	    db.session.refresh(role)
297	
298	    return jsonify({
299	        'status': 'ok',
300	        'data': _serialize_role(role),
301	    }), 200
302	
303	
304	# ============================================================
305	# POST /users/<id>/roles -- Assign Role to User
306	# ============================================================
307	
308	
309	@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles', methods=['POST'])
310	@check_permission('role.manage')
311	def assign_role_to_user(user_id):
312	    """POST /users/<id>/roles -- Assign a role to a user.
313	
314	    Request body (JSON):
315	        {"role_id": int}
316	
317	    Success (200):
318	        {"status": "ok", "data": {"message": "Role assigned"}}
319	
320	    Errors:
321	        403 -- Insufficient permissions or cross-tenant assignment
322	        404 -- User or role not found
323	    """
324	    # Step 1: Validate user exists and belongs to same tenant
325	    user = db.session.get(User, user_id)
326	    if user is None:
327	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404
328	
329	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
330	    if user.tenant_id != tenant_id:
331	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404
332	
333	    # Step 2: Parse and validate request body
334	    body = request.get_json(silent=True)
335	    if not isinstance(body, dict) or 'role_id' not in body:
336	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing role_id'}), 400
337	
338	    role_id = body.get('role_id')
339	    if not isinstance(role_id, int):
340	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'role_id must be an integer'}), 400
341	
342	    # Step 3: Validate role exists and belongs to same tenant
343	    role = db.session.get(Role, role_id)
344	    if role is None:
345	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404
346	
347	    if role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
348	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404
349	
350	    # Step 4: Insert the user-role association (ignore duplicates silently)
351	    db.session.execute(
352	        user_roles.insert().values(user_id=user_id, role_id=role_id)
353	    )
354	    db.session.commit()
355	
356	    return jsonify({
357	        'status': 'ok',
358	        'data': {'message': 'Role assigned'},
359	    }), 200
360	
361	
362	# ============================================================
363	# DELETE /users/<id>/roles/<role_id> -- Remove Role from User
364	# ============================================================
365	
366	
367	@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles/<int:role_id>', methods=['DELETE'])
368	@check_permission('role.manage')
369	def remove_role_from_user(user_id, role_id):
370	    """DELETE /users/<id>/roles/<role_id> -- Remove a role from a user.
371	
372	    This is an idempotent operation: removing a role that was never assigned
373	    still returns 200.
374	
375	    Success (200):
376	        {"status": "ok", "data": {"message": "Role removed"}}
377	
378	    Errors:
379	        403 -- Insufficient permissions
380	        404 -- User not found
381	    """
382	    # Step 1: Validate user exists and belongs to same tenant
383	    user = db.session.get(User, user_id)
384	    if user is None:
385	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404
386	
387	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
388	    if user.tenant_id != tenant_id:
389	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404
390	
391	    # Step 2: Delete the user-role association (idempotent -- no error if not found)
392	    db.session.execute(
393	        user_roles.delete().where(
394	            and_(
395	                user_roles.c.user_id == user_id,
396	                user_roles.c.role_id == role_id,
397	            )
398	        )
399	    )
400	    db.session.commit()
401	
402	    return jsonify({
403	        'status': 'ok',
404	        'data': {'message': 'Role removed'},
405	    }), 200
406

"""
T2 RBAC System -- Role Management Routes (Gen 1 Sample 1 Variant)

Evolutionary approach: recursive cycle detection, independent permission
resolution helper, batch permission insertion via association table inserts.

Register as a Flask Blueprint named 'role_bp'.
All operations require role.manage permission.

Key design decisions (Sample 1 signature):
- Cycle detection uses a recursive _would_create_cycle() function that
  walks the parent chain from new_parent_id looking for role_id
- Permission resolution is extracted into _resolve_permissions() which
  maps permission codes to Permission IDs with validation
- Batch inserts via db.session.execute(role_permissions.insert())
- Helper functions return (data, data, error_response) tuples for clean
  separation of validation from business logic
"""

from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g
from sqlalchemy import select, and_

from models import Role, Permission, User, role_permissions, user_roles
from middleware import check_permission
from app import db

role_bp = Blueprint('role_bp', __name__)


# ============================================================
# Helper Functions
# ============================================================


def _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
    """Check if setting new_parent_id as parent of role_id would create a cycle.

    Walks up the parent chain from new_parent_id. If we encounter role_id,
    then making new_parent_id the parent of role_id would form a cycle.

    Args:
        role_id: The role being updated (the potential child).
        new_parent_id: The proposed new parent role.

    Returns:
        True if a cycle would be created, False otherwise.
    """
    if new_parent_id is None:
        return False

    visited = set()
    current_id = new_parent_id

    while current_id is not None:
        if current_id == role_id:
            return True
        if current_id in visited:
            # Already visited -- should not happen in valid data, but safety guard
            return True
        visited.add(current_id)

        role = db.session.get(Role, current_id)
        if role is None:
            break
        current_id = role.parent_role_id

    return False


def _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id=None):
    """Map permission code strings to Permission model instances.

    Looks up each permission code in the database. Validates that all
    codes correspond to existing permissions.

    Args:
        perm_codes: List of permission code strings (e.g. ['doc.read', 'doc.write']).
        tenant_id: Unused parameter kept for API consistency with potential
                   future tenant-scoped permissions.

    Returns:
        (permissions, error_response) tuple.
        On success: (list of Permission objects, None)
        On failure: (None, (jsonify response, status_code))
    """
    if not isinstance(perm_codes, list):
        return None, (jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'permissions must be a list'}), 400)

    permissions = []
    for code in perm_codes:
        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
        if perm is None:
            return None, (
                jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': f'Unknown permission: {code}'}),
                400,
            )
        permissions.append(perm)

    return permissions, None


def _serialize_role(role):
    """Serialize a Role model instance into a JSON-safe dict.

    Includes id, name, tenant_id, parent_role_id, and permission codes.
    """
    return {
        'id': role.id,
        'name': role.name,
        'tenant_id': role.tenant_id,
        'parent_role_id': role.parent_role_id,
        'permissions': [p.code for p in role.permissions],
    }


def _get_current_tenant_id():
    """Extract tenant_id from the authenticated user stored in g."""
    return g.current_user.tenant_id


# ============================================================
# POST /roles -- Create Role
# ============================================================


@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['POST'])
@check_permission('role.manage')
def create_role():
    """POST /roles -- Create a new role within the current tenant.

    Request body (JSON):
        {"name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}

    Success (201):
        {"status": "ok", "data": {"id": int, "name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null,
                  "permissions": [str, ...]}}

    Errors:
        400 -- Missing name, invalid parent, or unknown permissions
        403 -- Insufficient permissions
        409 -- Duplicate role name within the same tenant
    """
    body = request.get_json(silent=True)

    # Step 1: Validate request body structure
    if not isinstance(body, dict):
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be a JSON object'}), 400

    name = body.get('name')
    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing or invalid name'}), 400

    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()

    # Step 2: Check for duplicate name within the same tenant
    existing = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name).first()
    if existing is not None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role name already exists in this tenant'}), 409

    # Step 3: Validate parent_role_id if provided
    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
    if parent_role_id is not None:
        parent_role = db.session.get(Role, parent_role_id)
        if parent_role is None:
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role not found'}), 400
        if parent_role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role must belong to the same tenant'}), 400

    # Step 4: Resolve permissions
    perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
    permissions, perm_error = _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id)
    if perm_error:
        return perm_error

    # Step 5: Create the role
    role = Role(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name, parent_role_id=parent_role_id)
    db.session.add(role)
    db.session.flush()  # Flush to get role.id for permission insertion

    # Step 6: Batch insert permissions via association table
    if permissions:
        db.session.execute(
            role_permissions.insert().values(
                [(role.id, p.id) for p in permissions]
            )
        )

    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': _serialize_role(role),
    }), 201


# ============================================================
# GET /roles -- List Roles
# ============================================================


@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['GET'])
@check_permission('role.manage')
def list_roles():
    """GET /roles -- List all roles for the current tenant.

    Success (200):
        {"status": "ok", "data": [{"id": int, "name": str, "tenant_id": int,
                  "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}, ...]}

    Errors:
        403 -- Insufficient permissions
    """
    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
    roles = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id).all()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': [_serialize_role(role) for role in roles],
    }), 200


# ============================================================
# PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Update Permissions & Parent
# ============================================================


@role_bp.route('/roles/<int:role_id>/permissions', methods=['PUT'])
@check_permission('role.manage')
def update_role_permissions(role_id):
    """PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Replace permission set and update parent.

    Request body (JSON):
        {"permissions": [str, ...], "parent_role_id": int|null}

    Success (200):
        {"status": "ok", "data": {"id": int, "name": str, "tenant_id": int,
                  "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}}

    Errors:
        400 -- Cycle detected, invalid parent, or unknown permissions
        403 -- Insufficient permissions
        404 -- Role not found
    """
    role = db.session.get(Role, role_id)
    if role is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    body = request.get_json(silent=True)

    # Step 1: Validate request body structure
    if not isinstance(body, dict):
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be a JSON object'}), 400

    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()

    # Step 2: Handle parent_role_id update if provided
    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
    if parent_role_id is not None:
        # Validate parent exists and belongs to same tenant
        parent_role = db.session.get(Role, parent_role_id)
        if parent_role is None:
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role not found'}), 400
        if parent_role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role must belong to the same tenant'}), 400

        # Check for inheritance cycle
        if _would_create_cycle(role_id, parent_role_id):
            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Setting this parent would create an inheritance cycle'}), 400

        role.parent_role_id = parent_role_id
    elif 'parent_role_id' in body:
        # Explicitly set to null if key is present but value is None
        role.parent_role_id = None

    # Step 3: Resolve new permissions if provided
    perm_codes = body.get('permissions')
    if perm_codes is not None:
        permissions, perm_error = _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id)
        if perm_error:
            return perm_error

        # Delete existing permissions and insert new ones
        db.session.execute(
            role_permissions.delete().where(role_permissions.c.role_id == role_id)
        )
        if permissions:
            db.session.execute(
                role_permissions.insert().values(
                    [(role_id, p.id) for p in permissions]
                )
            )

    db.session.commit()

    # Refresh role to pick up updated permissions
    db.session.refresh(role)

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': _serialize_role(role),
    }), 200


# ============================================================
# POST /users/<id>/roles -- Assign Role to User
# ============================================================


@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles', methods=['POST'])
@check_permission('role.manage')
def assign_role_to_user(user_id):
    """POST /users/<id>/roles -- Assign a role to a user.

    Request body (JSON):
        {"role_id": int}

    Success (200):
        {"status": "ok", "data": {"message": "Role assigned"}}

    Errors:
        403 -- Insufficient permissions or cross-tenant assignment
        404 -- User or role not found
    """
    # Step 1: Validate user exists and belongs to same tenant
    user = db.session.get(User, user_id)
    if user is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
    if user.tenant_id != tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    # Step 2: Parse and validate request body
    body = request.get_json(silent=True)
    if not isinstance(body, dict) or 'role_id' not in body:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing role_id'}), 400

    role_id = body.get('role_id')
    if not isinstance(role_id, int):
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'role_id must be an integer'}), 400

    # Step 3: Validate role exists and belongs to same tenant
    role = db.session.get(Role, role_id)
    if role is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    if role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404

    # Step 4: Insert the user-role association (ignore duplicates silently)
    db.session.execute(
        user_roles.insert().values(user_id=user_id, role_id=role_id)
    )
    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': {'message': 'Role assigned'},
    }), 200


# ============================================================
# DELETE /users/<id>/roles/<role_id> -- Remove Role from User
# ============================================================


@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles/<int:role_id>', methods=['DELETE'])
@check_permission('role.manage')
def remove_role_from_user(user_id, role_id):
    """DELETE /users/<id>/roles/<role_id> -- Remove a role from a user.

    This is an idempotent operation: removing a role that was never assigned
    still returns 200.

    Success (200):
        {"status": "ok", "data": {"message": "Role removed"}}

    Errors:
        403 -- Insufficient permissions
        404 -- User not found
    """
    # Step 1: Validate user exists and belongs to same tenant
    user = db.session.get(User, user_id)
    if user is None:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
    if user.tenant_id != tenant_id:
        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404

    # Step 2: Delete the user-role association (idempotent -- no error if not found)
    db.session.execute(
        user_roles.delete().where(
            and_(
                user_roles.c.user_id == user_id,
                user_roles.c.role_id == role_id,
            )
        )
    )
    db.session.commit()

    return jsonify({
        'status': 'ok',
        'data': {'message': 'Role removed'},
    }), 200

input.prompt:
你是 Tester 子 Agent。

请为 ATU-003 编写完整的单元测试文件。

**项目背景**：
- 这是一个 Flask RBAC 权限管理系统
- 工作目录: `/Users/harry/Workspaces/disclaude/workspace/mem-agent-pm/experiment/results/evolutionary_t2_rbac_2/workspace/`
- starter/ 目录包含源代码，tests/ 目录包含测试
- 已完成模块: `app.py` (Flask app factory), `models.py` (数据模型), `middleware.py` (JWT验证+权限检查), `routes_auth.py` (login接口), `routes_role.py` (角色管理)
- 需要测试的模块: `routes_document.py` (目前只有 Blueprint 定义，TODO 所有路由)

**ATU-003 功能描述**:
实现文档 CRUD 路由，使用 `document_bp = Blueprint('document_bp', __name__)`

接口列表:
1. **GET /documents** — 列出文档（需要 `doc.read` 权限，只返回本 tenant）
   - Response 200: {"status": "ok", "data": [{"id": int, "tenant_id": int, "owner_id": int, "title": str, "content": str}, ...]}

2. **GET /documents/<id>** — 获取单个文档（需要 `doc.read` + 同 tenant）
   - Response 200: 同上格式
   - 404: 文档不存在或不同 tenant

3. **POST /documents** — 创建文档（需要 `doc.write` 权限）
   - Request: {"title": str, "content": str}
   - Response 201: {"status": "ok", "data": {"id": int, "tenant_id": int, "owner_id": int, "title": str, "content": str}}
   - tenant_id 从 JWT 获取，owner_id = 当前用户

4. **PUT /documents/<id>** — 更新文档（需要 `doc.write` + owner 或 `doc.write.any` + 同 tenant）
   - Request: {"title": str, "content": str}
   - 404: 文档不存在
   - 403: 不是 owner 且无 doc.write.any

5. **DELETE /documents/<id>** — 删除文档（需要 `doc.delete` 权限 + 同 tenant）
   - 404: 文档不存在
   - 403: 无权限

**测试要求**：
1. 测试文件保存为: `tests/test_atu003_document.py`
2. 必须覆盖：
   - 成功列出文档（200），验证只返回本 tenant
   - 无 doc.read 权限返回 403
   - 成功获取单个文档（200）
   - 获取不存在文档返回 404
   - 跨 tenant 获取文档返回 404
   - 成功创建文档（201），验证 tenant_id 和 owner_id 正确
   - 无 doc.write 权限返回 403
   - 成功更新自己的文档（200）
   - 非 owner 且无 doc.write.any 更新他人文档返回 403
   - 有 doc.write.any 更新他人文档返回 200
   - 成功删除文档（200）
   - 无 doc.delete 权限返回 403
   - 跨 tenant 删除文档返回 404
3. 使用 pytest fixture 创建测试客户端和种子数据
4. 种子数据：两个 tenant, 多个 user (含 admin), 权限, 角色（admin 拥有所有权限）
5. 需要 ability to create roles, assign roles, and login to test RBAC enforcement
6. 使用 `sys.path.insert(0, ...)` 确保导入 starter 模块
7. 使用内存 SQLite 数据库

**重要参考**:
- 阅读 `tests/test_basic.py` 了解 fixture、_login、_create_role 辅助函数的风格
- 阅读 `starter/middleware.py` 了解 `check_permission` 装饰器
- 阅读 `starter/models.py` 了解 Document 模型
- 阅读 `starter/routes_role.py` 了解角色创建 API
- 阅读 `starter/routes_auth.py` 了解 login API

**关键**: 测试需要通过 login 获取 token，通过创建角色+分配角色来设置不同的权限场景。

测试应该能独立运行: `cd /Users/harry/Workspaces/disclaude/workspace/mem-agent-pm/experiment/results/evolutionary_t2_rbac_2/workspace && python -m pytest tests/test_atu003_document.py -v`

注意：此时 routes_document.py 的路由还未实现，测试预期会失败。但测试文件本身应该能被 pytest 正确发现和执行。

content.content:
1	"""
2	T2 RBAC System — Data Models
3	
4	Define SQLAlchemy models here.
5	Requirements:
6	- Tenant: id, name
7	- User: id, tenant_id (FK), username (unique), password_hash, created_at
8	- Role: id, tenant_id (FK), name, parent_role_id (nullable self-ref FK)
9	- Permission: id, code (e.g. doc.read, doc.write, doc.delete, doc.write.any, role.manage)
10	- role_permissions: many-to-many (role_id, permission_id)
11	- user_roles: many-to-many (user_id, role_id)
12	- Document: id, tenant_id (FK), owner_id (FK to User), title, content
13	"""
14	
15	from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
16	from datetime import datetime
17	import sys
18	import os
19	sys.path.insert(0, os.path.dirname(__file__))
20	
21	from app import db
22	
23	# Many-to-many association tables
24	role_permissions = db.Table('role_permissions',
25	    db.Column('role_id', db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('role.id'), primary_key=True),
26	    db.Column('permission_id', db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('permission.id'), primary_key=True)
27	)
28	
29	user_roles = db.Table('user_roles',
30	    db.Column('user_id', db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('user.id'), primary_key=True),
31	    db.Column('role_id', db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('role.id'), primary_key=True)
32	)
33	
34	
35	class Tenant(db.Model):
36	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
37	    name = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=False)
38	    users = db.relationship('User', backref='tenant', lazy=True)
39	    roles = db.relationship('Role', backref='tenant', lazy=True)
40	    documents = db.relationship('Document', backref='tenant', lazy=True)
41	
42	
43	class User(db.Model):
44	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
45	    tenant_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey('tenant.id'), nullable=False)
46	    username = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True, nullable=False)
47	    password_hash = db.Column(db.String(256), nullable=False)
48	    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
49	    roles = db.relationship('Role', secondary=user_roles, backref='users')

1	"""
2	T2 RBAC System -- Role Management Routes (Gen 1 Sample 1 Variant)
3	
4	Evolutionary approach: recursive cycle detection, independent permission
5	resolution helper, batch permission insertion via association table inserts.
6	
7	Register as a Flask Blueprint named 'role_bp'.
8	All operations require role.manage permission.
9	
10	Key design decisions (Sample 1 signature):
11	- Cycle detection uses a recursive _would_create_cycle() function that
12	  walks the parent chain from new_parent_id looking for role_id
13	- Permission resolution is extracted into _resolve_permissions() which
14	  maps permission codes to Permission IDs with validation
15	- Batch inserts via db.session.execute(role_permissions.insert())
16	- Helper functions return (data, data, error_response) tuples for clean
17	  separation of validation from business logic
18	"""
19	
20	from flask import Blueprint, request, jsonify, g
21	from sqlalchemy import select, and_
22	
23	from models import Role, Permission, User, role_permissions, user_roles
24	from middleware import check_permission
25	from app import db
26	
27	role_bp = Blueprint('role_bp', __name__)
28	
29	
30	# ============================================================
31	# Helper Functions
32	# ============================================================
33	
34	
35	def _would_create_cycle(role_id, new_parent_id):
36	    """Check if setting new_parent_id as parent of role_id would create a cycle.
37	
38	    Walks up the parent chain from new_parent_id. If we encounter role_id,
39	    then making new_parent_id the parent of role_id would form a cycle.
40	
41	    Args:
42	        role_id: The role being updated (the potential child).
43	        new_parent_id: The proposed new parent role.
44	
45	    Returns:
46	        True if a cycle would be created, False otherwise.
47	    """
48	    if new_parent_id is None:
49	        return False
50	
51	    visited = set()
52	    current_id = new_parent_id
53	
54	    while current_id is not None:
55	        if current_id == role_id:
56	            return True
57	        if current_id in visited:
58	            # Already visited -- should not happen in valid data, but safety guard
59	            return True
60	        visited.add(current_id)
61	
62	        role = db.session.get(Role, current_id)
63	        if role is None:
64	            break
65	        current_id = role.parent_role_id
66	
67	    return False
68	
69	
70	def _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id=None):
71	    """Map permission code strings to Permission model instances.
72	
73	    Looks up each permission code in the database. Validates that all
74	    codes correspond to existing permissions.
75	
76	    Args:
77	        perm_codes: List of permission code strings (e.g. ['doc.read', 'doc.write']).
78	        tenant_id: Unused parameter kept for API consistency with potential
79	                   future tenant-scoped permissions.
80	
81	    Returns:
82	        (permissions, error_response) tuple.
83	        On success: (list of Permission objects, None)
84	        On failure: (None, (jsonify response, status_code))
85	    """
86	    if not isinstance(perm_codes, list):
87	        return None, (jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'permissions must be a list'}), 400)
88	
89	    permissions = []
90	    for code in perm_codes:
91	        perm = Permission.query.filter_by(code=code).first()
92	        if perm is None:
93	            return None, (
94	                jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': f'Unknown permission: {code}'}),
95	                400,
96	            )
97	        permissions.append(perm)
98	
99	    return permissions, None
100	
101	
102	def _serialize_role(role):
103	    """Serialize a Role model instance into a JSON-safe dict.
104	
105	    Includes id, name, tenant_id, parent_role_id, and permission codes.
106	    """
107	    return {
108	        'id': role.id,
109	        'name': role.name,
110	        'tenant_id': role.tenant_id,
111	        'parent_role_id': role.parent_role_id,
112	        'permissions': [p.code for p in role.permissions],
113	    }
114	
115	
116	def _get_current_tenant_id():
117	    """Extract tenant_id from the authenticated user stored in g."""
118	    return g.current_user.tenant_id
119	
120	
121	# ============================================================
122	# POST /roles -- Create Role
123	# ============================================================
124	
125	
126	@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['POST'])
127	@check_permission('role.manage')
128	def create_role():
129	    """POST /roles -- Create a new role within the current tenant.
130	
131	    Request body (JSON):
132	        {"name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}
133	
134	    Success (201):
135	        {"status": "ok", "data": {"id": int, "name": str, "parent_role_id": int|null,
136	                  "permissions": [str, ...]}}
137	
138	    Errors:
139	        400 -- Missing name, invalid parent, or unknown permissions
140	        403 -- Insufficient permissions
141	        409 -- Duplicate role name within the same tenant
142	    """
143	    body = request.get_json(silent=True)
144	
145	    # Step 1: Validate request body structure
146	    if not isinstance(body, dict):
147	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be a JSON object'}), 400
148	
149	    name = body.get('name')
150	    if not name or not isinstance(name, str):
151	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing or invalid name'}), 400
152	
153	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
154	
155	    # Step 2: Check for duplicate name within the same tenant
156	    existing = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name).first()
157	    if existing is not None:
158	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role name already exists in this tenant'}), 409
159	
160	    # Step 3: Validate parent_role_id if provided
161	    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
162	    if parent_role_id is not None:
163	        parent_role = db.session.get(Role, parent_role_id)
164	        if parent_role is None:
165	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role not found'}), 400
166	        if parent_role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
167	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role must belong to the same tenant'}), 400
168	
169	    # Step 4: Resolve permissions
170	    perm_codes = body.get('permissions', [])
171	    permissions, perm_error = _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id)
172	    if perm_error:
173	        return perm_error
174	
175	    # Step 5: Create the role
176	    role = Role(tenant_id=tenant_id, name=name, parent_role_id=parent_role_id)
177	    db.session.add(role)
178	    db.session.flush()  # Flush to get role.id for permission insertion
179	
180	    # Step 6: Batch insert permissions via association table
181	    if permissions:
182	        db.session.execute(
183	            role_permissions.insert().values(
184	                [(role.id, p.id) for p in permissions]
185	            )
186	        )
187	
188	    db.session.commit()
189	
190	    return jsonify({
191	        'status': 'ok',
192	        'data': _serialize_role(role),
193	    }), 201
194	
195	
196	# ============================================================
197	# GET /roles -- List Roles
198	# ============================================================
199	
200	
201	@role_bp.route('/roles', methods=['GET'])
202	@check_permission('role.manage')
203	def list_roles():
204	    """GET /roles -- List all roles for the current tenant.
205	
206	    Success (200):
207	        {"status": "ok", "data": [{"id": int, "name": str, "tenant_id": int,
208	                  "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}, ...]}
209	
210	    Errors:
211	        403 -- Insufficient permissions
212	    """
213	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
214	    roles = Role.query.filter_by(tenant_id=tenant_id).all()
215	
216	    return jsonify({
217	        'status': 'ok',
218	        'data': [_serialize_role(role) for role in roles],
219	    }), 200
220	
221	
222	# ============================================================
223	# PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Update Permissions & Parent
224	# ============================================================
225	
226	
227	@role_bp.route('/roles/<int:role_id>/permissions', methods=['PUT'])
228	@check_permission('role.manage')
229	def update_role_permissions(role_id):
230	    """PUT /roles/<id>/permissions -- Replace permission set and update parent.
231	
232	    Request body (JSON):
233	        {"permissions": [str, ...], "parent_role_id": int|null}
234	
235	    Success (200):
236	        {"status": "ok", "data": {"id": int, "name": str, "tenant_id": int,
237	                  "parent_role_id": int|null, "permissions": [str, ...]}}
238	
239	    Errors:
240	        400 -- Cycle detected, invalid parent, or unknown permissions
241	        403 -- Insufficient permissions
242	        404 -- Role not found
243	    """
244	    role = db.session.get(Role, role_id)
245	    if role is None:
246	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404
247	
248	    body = request.get_json(silent=True)
249	
250	    # Step 1: Validate request body structure
251	    if not isinstance(body, dict):
252	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Request body must be a JSON object'}), 400
253	
254	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
255	
256	    # Step 2: Handle parent_role_id update if provided
257	    parent_role_id = body.get('parent_role_id')
258	    if parent_role_id is not None:
259	        # Validate parent exists and belongs to same tenant
260	        parent_role = db.session.get(Role, parent_role_id)
261	        if parent_role is None:
262	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role not found'}), 400
263	        if parent_role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
264	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Parent role must belong to the same tenant'}), 400
265	
266	        # Check for inheritance cycle
267	        if _would_create_cycle(role_id, parent_role_id):
268	            return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Setting this parent would create an inheritance cycle'}), 400
269	
270	        role.parent_role_id = parent_role_id
271	    elif 'parent_role_id' in body:
272	        # Explicitly set to null if key is present but value is None
273	        role.parent_role_id = None
274	
275	    # Step 3: Resolve new permissions if provided
276	    perm_codes = body.get('permissions')
277	    if perm_codes is not None:
278	        permissions, perm_error = _resolve_permissions(perm_codes, tenant_id)
279	        if perm_error:
280	            return perm_error
281	
282	        # Delete existing permissions and insert new ones
283	        db.session.execute(
284	            role_permissions.delete().where(role_permissions.c.role_id == role_id)
285	        )
286	        if permissions:
287	            db.session.execute(
288	                role_permissions.insert().values(
289	                    [(role_id, p.id) for p in permissions]
290	                )
291	            )
292	
293	    db.session.commit()
294	
295	    # Refresh role to pick up updated permissions
296	    db.session.refresh(role)
297	
298	    return jsonify({
299	        'status': 'ok',
300	        'data': _serialize_role(role),
301	    }), 200
302	
303	
304	# ============================================================
305	# POST /users/<id>/roles -- Assign Role to User
306	# ============================================================
307	
308	
309	@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles', methods=['POST'])
310	@check_permission('role.manage')
311	def assign_role_to_user(user_id):
312	    """POST /users/<id>/roles -- Assign a role to a user.
313	
314	    Request body (JSON):
315	        {"role_id": int}
316	
317	    Success (200):
318	        {"status": "ok", "data": {"message": "Role assigned"}}
319	
320	    Errors:
321	        403 -- Insufficient permissions or cross-tenant assignment
322	        404 -- User or role not found
323	    """
324	    # Step 1: Validate user exists and belongs to same tenant
325	    user = db.session.get(User, user_id)
326	    if user is None:
327	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404
328	
329	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
330	    if user.tenant_id != tenant_id:
331	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404
332	
333	    # Step 2: Parse and validate request body
334	    body = request.get_json(silent=True)
335	    if not isinstance(body, dict) or 'role_id' not in body:
336	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Missing role_id'}), 400
337	
338	    role_id = body.get('role_id')
339	    if not isinstance(role_id, int):
340	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'role_id must be an integer'}), 400
341	
342	    # Step 3: Validate role exists and belongs to same tenant
343	    role = db.session.get(Role, role_id)
344	    if role is None:
345	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404
346	
347	    if role.tenant_id != tenant_id:
348	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'Role not found'}), 404
349	
350	    # Step 4: Insert the user-role association (ignore duplicates silently)
351	    db.session.execute(
352	        user_roles.insert().values(user_id=user_id, role_id=role_id)
353	    )
354	    db.session.commit()
355	
356	    return jsonify({
357	        'status': 'ok',
358	        'data': {'message': 'Role assigned'},
359	    }), 200
360	
361	
362	# ============================================================
363	# DELETE /users/<id>/roles/<role_id> -- Remove Role from User
364	# ============================================================
365	
366	
367	@role_bp.route('/users/<int:user_id>/roles/<int:role_id>', methods=['DELETE'])
368	@check_permission('role.manage')
369	def remove_role_from_user(user_id, role_id):
370	    """DELETE /users/<id>/roles/<role_id> -- Remove a role from a user.
371	
372	    This is an idempotent operation: removing a role that was never assigned
373	    still returns 200.
374	
375	    Success (200):
376	        {"status": "ok", "data": {"message": "Role removed"}}
377	
378	    Errors:
379	        403 -- Insufficient permissions
380	        404 -- User not found
381	    """
382	    # Step 1: Validate user exists and belongs to same tenant
383	    user = db.session.get(User, user_id)
384	    if user is None:
385	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404
386	
387	    tenant_id = _get_current_tenant_id()
388	    if user.tenant_id != tenant_id:
389	        return jsonify({'status': 'error', 'message': 'User not found'}), 404
390	
391	    # Step 2: Delete the user-role association (idempotent -- no error if not found)
392	    db.session.execute(
393	        user_roles.delete().where(
394	            and_(
395	                user_roles.c.user_id == user_id,
396	                user_roles.c.role_id == role_id,
397	            )
398	        )
399	    )
400	    db.session.commit()
401	
402	    return jsonify({
403	        'status': 'ok',
404	        'data': {'message': 'Role removed'},
405	    }), 200

3	
4	This file sets up the Flask app and database. DO NOT MODIFY this file.
5	All business logic goes in the other modules.
6	"""

9	from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
10	import os

15	def create_app():
16	    app = Flask(__name__)
17	    app.config['SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI'] = 'sqlite:///rbac.db'
18	    app.config['SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS'] = False
19	    app.config['JWT_SECRET'] = os.environ.get('JWT_SECRET', 'dev-secret-key')

22	    db.init_app(app)

25	    from models import Tenant, User, Role, Permission, Document
26	
27	    # Import and register route blueprints
28	    from routes_auth import auth_bp

30	    from routes_role import role_bp
31	    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp)
32	    app.register_blueprint(document_bp)
33	    app.register_blueprint(role_bp)

35	    with app.app_context():
36	        db.create_all()
37	
38	    return app
39	
40
